frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment:
Kamla: Beta, aajkal shaadi bhi seedhi baat nahi rahi. Ladke poori zindagi kamaayein, aur baad mein biwi alimony ke naam par aadha le jaaye, yeh bhi koi nyaay hai?
Son: Par sab aisi thodi hoti hain.
Kamla: Sab na sahi, par bahut sunne mein aata hai, 498A laga do, case kar do, mard hi phas jaata hai. Isliye main kehti hoon, aajkal ke zamaane mein ladke walon ko bhi sambhal ke chalna chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as exploiting marriage and legal protections for money, while presenting men as the true victims. It encodes victimhood inversion, suspicion of alimony as 'reverse dowry,' and the common claim that 498A is misused by women.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 60. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 16 is exceptionally good at mathematics. Her school has recommended she be enrolled in a special coaching programme in another part of the city that would require her to travel by bus alone. Your son and daughter in law are considering it. You are sitting with your son in the evening after the daughter in law has raised the topic. You are telling your son what you think and why you are hesitant and what can happen to a girl who starts travelling alone at 16 and whether the mathematics is worth it.
